entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: Country where the study on sustainable farming is conducted

entities.name: MAHARASHTRA
entities.description: State in India where the study is focused

entities.name: LATUR
entities.description: District in Maharashtra characterized by traditional farming and cash crops

entities.name: SOLAPUR
entities.description: District in Maharashtra characterized by diverse cropping patterns including horticulture

entities.name: SOYBEAN
entities.description: Cash crop grown in the Latur district

entities.name: SUGARCANE
entities.description: Cash crop grown in the Latur district

entities.name: GUAVA
entities.description: Horticultural crop grown in the Solapur district

entities.name: GRAPES
entities.description: Horticultural crop grown in the Solapur district

entities.name: CHRYSANTHEMUMS
entities.description: Floricultural crop grown in the Solapur district

entities.name: MULTIDIMENSIONAL SUSTAINABILITY ASSESSMENT TOOL
entities.description: A tool (MSAT) used to evaluate sustainability across economic, environmental, social, human well-being, and productivity dimensions

entities.name: STRATIFIED SAMPLING
entities.description: A sampling technique used to capture regional diversity and socio-economic challenges

entities.name: HOUSEHOLD CATEGORIZATION
entities.description: A process of grouping farm households into typologies based on socio-economic factors

entities.name: SUSTAINABLE FARMING PRACTICES
entities.description: Agricultural practices aimed at achieving long-term environmental, social, and economic viability

entities.name: FARM HOUSEHOLD TYPOLOGIES
entities.description: Classification of farm households based on resource access and socio-economic status

entities.name: LAND OWNERSHIP
entities.description: A socio-economic factor used to differentiate farm household types

entities.name: INCOME
entities.description: A socio-economic factor influencing sustainability performance and household categorization

entities.name: ACCESS TO IRRIGATION
entities.description: A resource-based factor used to differentiate farm household types

entities.name: ECONOMIC SUSTAINABILITY
entities.description: A dimension of sustainability evaluated by the MSAT tool

entities.name: ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY
entities.description: A dimension of sustainability evaluated by the MSAT tool

entities.name: SOCIAL SUSTAINABILITY
entities.description: A dimension of sustainability evaluated by the MSAT tool

entities.name: HUMAN WELL-BEING
entities.description: A dimension of sustainability evaluated by the MSAT tool

entities.name: PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: A dimension of sustainability evaluated by the MSAT tool

entities.name: WATER MANAGEMENT
entities.description: An area of sustainability where both Latur and Solapur districts performed reasonably well

entities.name: HEALTH
entities.description: An area of sustainability where both Latur and Solapur districts performed reasonably well

entities.name: ECONOMIC RESILIENCE
entities.description: A sustainability area identified as a major weakness in the studied districts

entities.name: BIODIVERSITY CONSERVATION
entities.description: A sustainability area identified as a major weakness in the studied districts

entities.name: EDUCATION
entities.description: A socio-economic factor that impacts the sustainability performance of farm households

entities.name: GENDER EQUITY
entities.description: A social factor identified as a gap that needs to be addressed for inclusive sustainability

entities.name: MIXED-FARMING SYSTEMS
entities.description: Agricultural systems combining different types of crops and livestock for which sustainability recommendations are provided

entities.name: INSTITUTIONAL CREDIT
entities.description: Financial support from formal institutions needed to improve farming sustainability

entities.name: BIODIVERSITY-FRIENDLY FARMING
entities.description: Agricultural practices that promote and protect biological diversity
